section_title: Where one person employs another to do an act which is criminal, the
content:
224. Where one person employs another to do an act which is criminal, the
of employer
employer is not liable to the agent, either upon an express or an implied
of agent to
do a promise, to indemnify him against the consequences of that Act.
criminal act
Illustrations
(a) A employs B to beat C, and agrees to indemnify him against all
consequences of act. B thereupon beats C, and has to pay damages to C for
so doing. A is not liable to indemnify B for those damages.
(b) B, the proprietor of a newspaper, publishes, at A's request, liable upon C
in the paper, and A agrees to indemnify B against the consequences of the
publication, and all costs and damages of any action in respect thereof. B is

sued by C and has to pay damages, and also incurs expenses. A is not liable
to B upon the indemnity.
Compensation
